section.title: Introduction and Overview
section.p: NanoColour is a module which renders syntax-coloured text from a caller-supplied memory buffer. It is intended for applications which need to size and draw source text or similarly structured files without embedding their own nanorc parser, colouriser or font management.

The module combines three pieces of work into a single service: syntax selection, colour decoding, and graphical rendering. A caller opens a rendering session for a text buffer, optionally supplies a syntax name, configures the rendering style, requests the overall rendered size, and then renders the text at a chosen position.
If no syntax name is supplied, the module searches the nanorc definitions in NanoRCs$Dir and selects the most suitable syntax automatically.

section.title: Technical details
section.p: Each open session is represented by an opaque handle returned by . The handle owns the decoded text, the selected syntax, the cached colour information, the chosen font details and any cached layout information needed for efficient measurement and redraw.

subsection.title: Rendering model
subsection.p: The module creates a single GContext during initialisation and uses it for all rendering operations. Callers do not supply a GContext handle. Instead, the session records the presentation choices which the module should apply when measuring and drawing.

The module owns the font selection for each session. The caller supplies a font name and font size through , and the module creates or replaces the underlying font instance as required. Changing the font, default colours or text content invalidates any cached layout metrics.

subsection.title: Syntax selection
subsection.p: If the syntax pointer passed to or is non-zero, it names the syntax to be used explicitly. If the pointer is zero, the module searches the nanorc files in NanoRCs$Dir and auto-detects a syntax from the supplied text.

Auto-detection is intended to follow the same syntax-matching rules as the NanoColour library: filename-style rules where available, followed by header matching against the start of the buffer. If no suitable syntax can be identified, the module reports an error rather than silently applying an arbitrary syntax.

subsection.title: Measurement and rendering
subsection.p: The session caches enough layout information that can be called before any drawing takes place. This allows applications to size work areas, scroll extents or transient display regions before invoking .

Rendering is performed relative to the caller-supplied origin. The draw operation uses the session's configured colours and font. An optional clip rectangle may be supplied to permit callers to redraw only the region which intersects the visible area.

subsection.title: Flags and handles
subsection.p: All SWIs use R0 for flags. Reserved flag bits must be zero. Session handles returned by the module are opaque values and must not be interpreted by the caller.

subsection.title: Provisional allocation
subsection.p: This design uses the provisional SWI chunk base &hex;C3AC0 and the provisional error base &hex;9FBA00. Both values are unallocated and are recorded as placeholders pending formal registration.

sysvar-definition.name: NanoRCs$Dir
sysvar-definition.description: Directory containing nanorc syntax definition files

use.p: This variable identifies the directory searched when syntax selection is requested without an explicit syntax name. The directory should contain nanorc files in the format understood by the NanoColour library.

swi-definition.name: NanoColour_Open
swi-definition.description: Creates a rendering session for a text buffer

entry.register-use: flags:
Pointer to the text buffer to be rendered
Length of the text buffer in bytes
Pointer to a NUL-terminated syntax name, or 0 to auto-detect from NanoRCs$Dir

use.p: This SWI creates a new rendering session from the caller-supplied buffer. The buffer is treated as the text source for the session, and the session retains whatever internal state is required to recolour, size and render that content.
If R3 is zero, the module performs syntax auto-detection using the definitions found in NanoRCs$Dir. If R3 is non-zero, the named syntax is used directly.

entry.register-use: flags:
Session handle

use.p: This SWI destroys a session created by and releases all associated state, including cached colour information, layout caches and module-owned font objects for the session.

swi-definition.name: NanoColour_SetStyle
swi-definition.description: Configures the module-owned font and default colours for a session

entry.register-use:
flags:
If set, replace the default foreground colour from R5 If set, replace the default background colour from R6 If set, replace the font name and size from R2-R4
Session handle
Pointer to a NUL-terminated font name; ignored unless bit 2 is set
Font width in sixteenths of a point; ignored unless bit 2 is set
Font height in sixteenths of a point; ignored unless bit 2 is set
Default foreground colour value; ignored unless bit 0 is set
Default background colour value; ignored unless bit 1 is set

use.p: This SWI updates the presentation for an existing session. The module owns the font instance created from the supplied name and size, and replaces any existing session font when the font-select flag is set.
Any change made by this SWI invalidates the cached layout metrics for the session, so that the next call to or recalculates the layout.

swi-definition.name: NanoColour_GetSize
swi-definition.description: Returns the rendered bounds for a session

entry.register-use: flags:
Session handle

use.p: This SWI returns the total bounds of the rendered text for the session's current style. Applications should call it before drawing if they need to establish the size of a work area or the extent of a display region.

swi-definition.name: NanoColour_Render
swi-definition.description: Renders the coloured text for a session

entry.register-use:
flags:
If set, R4 points to a clip rectangle
Session handle
X coordinate of the render origin
Y coordinate of the render origin
Pointer to a clip rectangle, or 0 if bit 0 is clear

use.p: This SWI renders the current contents of the session using the module-owned GContext and the session's configured style. The origin identifies the top left position of the rendered text.
If a clip rectangle is supplied, the module may reject rendering which falls wholly outside the rectangle and may use the rectangle to restrict redraw work.

swi-definition.name: NanoColour_Recolour
swi-definition.description: Replaces the session text and re-runs syntax colouring

entry.register-use: flags:
Session handle
Pointer to the replacement text buffer
Length of the replacement text buffer in bytes
Pointer to a NUL-terminated syntax name, or 0 to auto-detect from NanoRCs$Dir

use.p: This SWI updates an existing session with new text and re-runs the colouring stage. It is intended for viewers and editors which need to keep a stable handle while their text changes over time.
Changing the text or syntax also invalidates the cached layout metrics for the session.

section.title: Error messages
section.p: The error numbers in this section use the provisional unallocated base &hex;9FBA00 and are intended to be replaced only if a different registered base is assigned during implementation.

use.p: This error is reported when a SWI is called with one or more reserved bits set in R0.

error-definition.name: NanoColour_BadHandle
error-definition.description: The supplied session handle is not valid

use.p: This error is reported when a SWI which operates on an existing session is passed an unknown or expired handle.

error-definition.name: NanoColour_BadParameter
error-definition.description: A required pointer or size parameter was invalid

use.p: This error is reported when the caller supplies an invalid text pointer, an invalid length, or an invalid style parameter block for the requested operation.

error-definition.name: NanoColour_NoSyntax
error-definition.description: No suitable syntax could be found

use.p: This error is reported when the caller asks for auto-detection and the module cannot identify a suitable syntax from NanoRCs$Dir, or when the explicitly named syntax does not exist.

error-definition.name: NanoColour_FontNotFound
error-definition.description: The requested font could not be selected

use.p: This error is reported when the module cannot create or select the requested font for a session.

error-definition.name: NanoColour_NoMemory
error-definition.description: There was insufficient memory for the requested operation

use.p: This error is reported when the module cannot allocate the working storage needed for syntax processing, colour decoding, font state or rendering caches.

section.title: Examples
section.extended-example: handle = SWI "NanoColour_Open", flags 0, text_ptr, text_len, 0 SWI "NanoColour_SetStyle", set font and colours, handle, "Corpus.Medium", 192, 192, foreground, background width, height = SWI "NanoColour_GetSize", 0, handle SWI "NanoColour_Render", 0, handle, x, y, 0 SWI "NanoColour_Close", 0, handle

revision.author: Charles
revision.title: Renamed module and assigned provisional bases
revision.change: Renamed the module interface to NanoColour and documented provisional unallocated SWI and error bases.

revision.author: Charles
revision.title: Initial version
revision.change: Documented the proposed NanoColour module interface and its SWIs.
